title: Social Interaction
description: Rules and guidelines for character interactions with NPCs and other creatures

overview.description: During their adventures, player characters meet many different people and face some monsters that would rather talk than fight. In those situations, it's time for social interaction, which takes many forms.
examples: Try to convince a burglar to confess to wrongdoing
overview.dm_role: The Dungeon Master assumes the roles of any nonplayer characters who are participating

npc_attitudes.description: An NPC's attitude toward your character is Friendly, Indifferent, or Hostile, as defined in the Rules Glossary

attitudes.description: Friendly NPCs are predisposed to help
Neutral NPCs have no particular feelings toward the characters
attitudes.inclination: Requires persuasion or incentive to assist

attitudes.description: Hostile ones are inclined to hinder
attitudes.inclination: Actively opposes or obstructs the characters

interaction_methods.description: Social interactions progress in two ways: through roleplaying and ability checks

roleplaying.definition: Roleplaying is, literally, the act of playing out a role. In this case, it's you as a player determining how your character thinks, acts, and talks.
roleplaying.scope: Roleplaying is part of every aspect of the game, and it comes to the fore during social interactions

active_approach.description: Speak and act as your character would, using first person
active_approach.example: Speaking in character: 'I don't think we can trust this merchant.'

descriptive_approach.description: Describe what your character does and says without acting it out
descriptive_approach.example: Describing actions: 'My character tries to intimidate the guard by mentioning our powerful allies.'

dm_response.factors: The DM uses an NPC's personality and your character's actions and attitudes to determine how an NPC reacts

examples.effective_approach: Threats of imprisonment might make them buckle

examples.ineffective_approach: Refuses to help if the characters badger her

strategy_tips.observation: When interacting with an NPC, pay attention to the DM's portrayal of the NPC's personality
strategy_tips.information_gathering: You might be able to learn an NPC's goals and then use that information to influence the NPC
strategy_tips.positive_outcomes: If you offer NPCs something they want or play on their sympathies, fears, or goals, you can form friendships, ward off violence, or learn a key piece of information
strategy_tips.negative_outcomes: If you insult a proud warrior or speak ill of a noble's allies, your efforts to convince or deceive will likely fail

ability_checks.role: Ability checks can be key in determining the outcome of a social interaction
ability_checks.relationship_to_roleplay: Your roleplaying efforts can alter an NPC's attitude, but there might still be an element of chance if the DM wants dice to play a role in determining an NPC's response to you
ability_checks.typical_action: In such situations, the DM will typically ask you to take the Influence action

strategic_considerations.skill_awareness: Pay attention to your skill proficiencies when thinking of how you will interact with an NPC
strategic_considerations.team_approach: Use an approach that relies on your group's skill proficiencies
strategic_considerations.example: If the group needs to trick a guard into letting them into a castle, the Rogue who is proficient in Deception should lead the discussion

influence_action_skills.use: Tell convincing lies or maintain false identities
Use threats or imposing presence to coerce cooperation
Entertain, tell stories, or put on acts to influence mood
Honestly and graciously convince someone through logical argument

social_interaction_outcomes.attitude_changes: Successfully influencing NPCs can shift their attitude from Hostile to Indifferent or from Indifferent to Friendly
social_interaction_outcomes.information_gathering: Social interactions can reveal important plot information, rumors, or clues
social_interaction_outcomes.access_opportunities: Good social interactions can open doors (literally and figuratively) that might otherwise be closed
social_interaction_outcomes.conflict_avoidance: Successful social interaction can prevent combat encounters or reduce their severity
social_interaction_outcomes.resource_acquisition: NPCs might provide equipment, services, or assistance based on social interaction outcomes

common_scenarios.considerations: Price haggling, seeking information about goods, building merchant relationships
Gaining access, avoiding legal trouble, acquiring permits or information
Building rapport, buying drinks, entertaining patrons to loosen tongues
Avoiding combat, negotiating safe passage, finding common ground
